frame_analyses.description: Initial setup view showing FLS trainer with Penrose drain, suture material, and instruments positioned. Two green laparoscopic graspers visible in ports.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean setup with proper instrument positioning

frame_analyses.description: Trainee beginning needle loading with right hand grasper approaching the curved needle. Suture and needle positioned for pickup.
Needle being grasped and manipulated for proper loading. Both instruments active in coordinating needle positioning.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good bimanual coordination during needle loading

frame_analyses.description: Needle being driven through first mark on Penrose drain. Proper needle trajectory and penetration angle visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Accurate placement through first target mark

frame_analyses.description: Completing needle passage through second mark on drain. Suture being pulled through to achieve proper positioning.
Beginning first throw of surgeon's knot. Suture ends being manipulated to create initial double throw configuration.
Completing surgeon's knot (double throw) as first throw. Right hand dominant in throw execution.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean surgeon's knot formation with appropriate tension

frame_analyses.description: Transitioning to second throw with hand switching. Left hand now taking dominant role for single throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand switching demonstrated between throws

frame_analyses.description: Final positioning before cutting. Scissors approaching suture ends for final trimming inside trainer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate cutting preparation with proper instrument positioning

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot (double throw) executed with right hand dominance
Single throw with proper hand switching from right to left
Final single throw with hand switch back to right, completing secure knot

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit well-closed with no visible gaps, proper knot tension achieved

technique_summary: Excellent technical execution with proper surgeon's knot, correct hand switching sequence, and accurate suture placement. Clean, efficient performance consistent with advanced skill level.
improvement_suggestions: Continue maintaining this high level of precision
Focus on reducing any minor placement deviations
strengths: Perfect knot sequence with surgeon's knot and proper hand switching
Accurate suture placement with minimal deviation
Efficient completion time showing good skill progression
